meta.productNameNote: Placeholder — swap freely. Alternatives: Pinpoint, MapStamp, Latitag, GeoMark.
meta.tagline: Geotag your photos for Local SEO in seconds.
meta.oneLiner: Drop in photos, pin a location on the map, and we write accurate GPS + local-SEO metadata into every image — hundreds at a time.
meta.platform: Responsive web app (desktop-first workspace, mobile-capable marketing + light editing)
meta.primaryGoal: Beat GeoImgr on UX clarity, batch speed, and Local-SEO usefulness while matching its feature set.

lockedDecisions.workspaceLayout: C · Two-column studio (left column: photos stacked over metadata editor; right column: large map)
lockedDecisions.aesthetic: APIDistributed canonical identity — editorial charcoal + orange-red, dark-first

differentiators: Cleaner, faster map workspace — search, drag-pin, and reverse-geocode in one panel with no page reloads.
Per-photo location overrides + CSV bulk-assign (map different addresses to different photos in one batch).
Local-SEO metadata, not just GPS: writes IPTC/XMP keywords, caption, city/state/country, sublocation, and business name (NAP) alongside EXIF GPS.
Privacy-forward: processing happens in-browser by default; originals never leave the device unless the user opts in.
Built-in Geo Report: a verifiable map + EXIF table proving every photo is correctly tagged (great for SEO deliverables/clients).
Modern formats: JPG, PNG, WebP, plus HEIC ingest.
Transparent metered pricing with a live usage meter instead of opaque limits.
competitiveParity: Google Maps + Places autocomplete address search
Up to 1000 photos / user / month tiers
Self-serve invoices with EU VAT ID (reverse-charge, no VAT for valid EU VAT IDs)

targetUsers.need: Tag client photos with exact business location + geo keywords at scale; produce proof for clients.
Make Google Business Profile / website photos location-relevant without technical skills.
Batch-tag shoots by address quickly, keep originals safe.
Repeatable, fast workflow; clear metadata control.

designSystem.identity: APIDistributed canonical — Premium Editorial (charcoal + orange-red, NEXUM/Hexaon style). Dark-first and cinematic with big confident geometric type, ONE warm orange-red accent, generous space, faint bordered grid + film grain, frosted-glass panels, pill+circular-arrow CTAs, parenthetical uppercase eyebrows. Serious infrastructure with edge — never AI-slop.
designSystem.appliesTo: Use verbatim across every screen, dashboard, nav, footer, logo, email.

themes.rhythm: Marketing alternates dark hero → light content → dark CTA (Vercel/Linear cadence). Workspace stays dark-first (charcoal chrome) with a DARK-styled map so the map integrates instead of glaring.

semanticMapping.coordinatesAndExifValues: JetBrains Mono in accent or dark.textStrong

codeSyntax.note: Only for terminal/curl snippets — conventional code semantics, not brand color.

mono.use: Coordinates, EXIF values, license keys, file names, base URLs, console/meters

logo.mark: Orange-gradient rounded-square tile (9px radius) containing a dark forward-chevron '›' + a dark dot — connotes distribute / forward. Reskinned here as a chevron-over-pin for the geotagging product (optional).
logo.wordmark: Set in Archivo 700, with a small ™.
logo.appIcon: Dark rounded-square tile with the mark; must read at 16px.

surfaceTreatments: Faint film-grain / noise overlay on dark sections
6-column vertical grid rule overlay on dark sections
Faint GIANT background wordmark behind hero
Soft radial accent glow behind focal points

pillArrow.spec: .pill-arrow — fully-rounded pill with a leading circular-arrow icon that rotates -45deg on hover

components.cards: Rounded 12–16px, thin hairline borders, soft shadow; on dark use translucent glass surfaces.
components.nav: Charcoal; logo left, uppercase tracked links center, white pill-arrow CTA right; transparent over dark hero, solidifies on scroll. NO scroll-collapse wordmark animation.

principles: Map pin drops with a small bounce
No gimmicks, never clone another brand's signature motion

topBar.style: Charcoal (dark.base), hairline bottom border, faint grain.
center: primaryNav: ( WORKSPACE ) ( LIBRARY ) ( REPORTS ) — uppercase tracked

appShell.licenseKeyAffordance: Top-right 'Enter license key' pill-arrow button (mirrors GeoImgr) opening a modal with a JetBrains-Mono key field + instant validation state.
appShell.notes: Workspace is full-bleed dark. Account / Billing / Reports use a centered 1280px container that may switch to a light content surface per the dark→light rhythm.

appPrimary.label: Library
appPrimary.description: History of processed photos and batches

screens.rhythm: Dark hero → light how-it-works/features → dark CTA.

content.headline: Stamp the exact location into every photo.
content.sub: Search an address, drop your pin, and we write accurate GPS + local-SEO metadata into JPG, PNG, WebP and HEIC — hundreds at a time.

screens.purpose: THE core screen. Upload, locate, edit metadata, batch-process, download. This is where we beat GeoImgr.
screens.layoutModel: Two-column studio (LOCKED): full-bleed dark. LEFT column ~40% stacks a Photos panel over the Metadata editor; RIGHT column ~60% is a large DARK-styled map. Bottom-docked batch progress. Columns are independently scrollable; left column is collapsible to give the map more room.
screens.mapStyle: Dark map theme (Google Maps dark style or Mapbox dark) so it integrates with the charcoal chrome; accent-orange pin stands out.

leftColumnTop_Photos.purpose: Holds the working batch of photos.

copy.idle: Drag photos here or browse — JPG, PNG, WebP, HEIC

hover quick-actions: locate / remove / view EXIF

leftColumnBottom_Inspector.multiSelectBehavior: When multiple photos selected, fields show a 'Mixed' state and apply to all selected on save.

rightColumn_Map.purpose: Find and set the exact location.

actions: Set as default for new uploads

steps: Select photo B → set different pin

empty.workspace: Big dropzone with cartographic illustration + 'Drag a photo to begin'
empty.library: No batches yet — illustration + CTA to workspace

loading.pattern: Skeletons for cards/tables; map shows shimmer; per-file progress in panel

error.perPhoto: Red status badge + tooltip reason (unsupported, corrupt, no GPS write)
error.global: Inline banner with retry; never lose the user's batch

success.pattern: Status badges flip to 'Tagged'; success toast; download CTA surfaces

limitReached.pattern: Usage meter turns warning; modal to upgrade seats/plan; never silently fail

workspace.tablet: map on top + collapsible photos/metadata drawer below

responsive.marketing: Fully responsive; hero stacks; comparison table becomes scrollable cards on mobile.

requirements: Keyboard-operable map pin (arrow-key nudge + coordinate input fallback)
Color is never the only status signal — pair badges with icon+label
Alt text on photo thumbnails = filename
Reduced-motion variant for pin drop / progress / grain drift
Form labels + aria-live for batch progress and toasts
Min contrast 4.5:1 for body text on charcoal/paper

microcopy.dropzone: Drag photos here or browse — JPG, PNG, WebP, HEIC
microcopy.applyAll: Apply this location to all photos
microcopy.vat: Valid EU VAT ID? VAT is reverse-charged — you pay no VAT.

designAgentInstructions: Design the desktop two-column workspace FIRST — it's the product. Left column = Photos over Metadata; right column = large DARK map.

Use the APIDistributed canonical identity verbatim: charcoal #17171A base, ONE orange-red accent #FF4D1C, Archivo display, Hanken Grotesk body, JetBrains Mono for all technical values. Grain + 6-col grid + glass panels for depth. Pill+circular-arrow CTAs. Parenthetical uppercase eyebrows.
Style the map DARK so it integrates with the charcoal chrome; the accent pin must pop.
Every photo shows clear tag status at a glance (icon+label+color); batch progress is always visible while processing.
Show, don't tell, the differentiators: per-photo overrides, CSV assign, SEO-metadata tab, Geo Report.
Keep the 'Enter license key' pill-arrow top-right to match GeoImgr expectations.
Avoid AI-slop: no serif display, no purple gradients, no competing accents, no timid type.

openAssumptions: Pricing numbers are placeholders pending your decision.
HEIC is ingest-only (converted on export); confirm if you want HEIC output.
Default processing is in-browser/client-side for privacy; a cloud-sync option is mentioned but optional.
